displayName: Threshing — By Hand vs By Machine
description: Class 6 Science interactive (NCERT Curiosity) comparing threshing — separating grain from harvested stalks — done by hand versus by machine. Four sections: Tools (animated by-hand / by-machine demos plus a drag-to-sort Compare board), Learn (concept card walk with a comparison table), Real World (where threshing happens, from a handful to a whole farm), and Free Play. Celebration intensity and copy scale by grade tier (6 most playful, 8 most formal).
subject: Science
topic: Threshing — separating grain from stalks, by hand vs by machine
concepts: Separation of grain from harvested crop
Choosing a method by scale, cost, and effort

1.1.0: Synced JSON to the actual component contract: real sections (tools/learn/real_world/free_play) and real props (initialSection, initialMethod, showSectionNav).
Removed stale/incorrect fields (initialMode, showModeSelector, hand/machine card sides).
Added additionalProps schema + examples; the component now also reads config from props.additionalProps, matching the other tools.
Added exportName, designSystem, agentQuickReference, and NCERT alignment.

width.description: Maximum width in pixels. The tool is responsive and self-sizes up to this width.

height.description: Optional minimum height in pixels. 0 = let content determine height (recommended).

gradeLevel.description: Grade tier. Controls celebration intensity (confetti count/duration) and encouragement copy. 6 = most playful, 8 = lightest/most formal.

initialSection.description: Which section the tool opens on.

initialMethod.description: Which method demo the Tools section opens on.

showSectionNav.description: Show/hide the Tools / Learn / Real World / Free Play section tabs. Hide to lock the tool to a single section.

showPlayPause.description: Show/hide the play/pause control on the Tools-section demos.

additionalProps.description: Agent config may be passed nested here (same pattern as the other tools). Values here override the top-level parameters of the same name. Step copy and Compare-board cards are defined internally and are not agent-configurable.

tools.title: Tools — Explore
tools.intro: Explore — see threshing in action before we name the parts.
tools.description: An animated 4-step demo of the chosen method (By Hand or By Machine), switchable, followed by a side-by-side CompareView and a drag-to-sort Compare board. The student drags four statement cards onto the By Hand or By Machine side; correct drops lock with a reason, wrong drops bounce back with a hint, and completing it triggers confetti and a summary.

compareBoard.description: Drag each card to the side it best fits. Each card is correct for exactly one side.

cards.text: Threshes a whole field in a day

learn.title: Learn
learn.intro: Learn — one idea at a time, each building on the last.
learn.description: A guided card walk introducing threshing concepts one step at a time; the final card embeds a CompareTable summarising hand vs machine across cost, fuel, speed, and amount-per-day.

real_world.title: Real World
real_world.intro: Real world — where threshing happens, from a handful to a whole farm.
real_world.description: Card walk of real settings, each tagged with the scale (small household amount up to whole-farm) at which that method is used.

free_play.title: Free Play
free_play.intro: Free play — experiment on your own.
free_play.description: An open sandbox where students experiment without a fixed step sequence. Celebration intensity follows the grade tier.

components: Pill UIButton kit (contained / highlight / outlined / text)
Inline stroke icons (no external icon library)
designSystem.notes: Imports only react — no external dependencies, no Tailwind CDN.

teachConcept.description: Full flow: open on the Tools demos, let students explore then sort the Compare board.

conceptCardsOnly.description: Open on the Learn card walk for a guided explanation.

compareActivity.description: Lock to the Tools section (no tabs) for the drag-to-sort Compare activity.

ncertAlignment.keyConceptTaught: Threshing separates grain from the cut, dried stalks of a harvested crop. It can be done by hand (beating bundles against a hard surface) or by machine (a powered drum). Both do the same job; the better choice depends on the amount, cost, and effort.
learningObjectives: Define threshing as separating grain from harvested stalks
Describe the by-hand method and the by-machine method
Compare the two methods by amount, cost, fuel, and effort
Justify which method suits a given situation (small amount vs whole field)
Connect threshing to other separation methods in the chapter
